name: Springfield Metro
free_text: Springfield has a mature fiber network, a university partnership producing 800 CS graduates per year, and an established open-data portal with 5 years of city datasets. The city council passed an AI ethics resolution in 2023.

expected_outcome.notes: High scores across all dimensions. Path A and Path B should align. Use this profile first to show the system working smoothly.

name: Maplewood Township
free_text: Maplewood has adopted an AI policy on paper but implementation is inconsistent across departments. The IT department has 3 staff. Most data is siloed in legacy systems with no API access. Neighboring towns have implemented similar policies with mixed results.

expected_outcome.notes: DIVERGENCE TRIGGER PROFILE. Governance scores high on paper (58) because the policy exists. The LLM should recognize from case studies that suburban communities with policy-on-paper often score 20-30 points lower in practice. This is the dramatic demo moment — use this second.

name: Clearwater Rural District
free_text: Clearwater covers a 600 square mile area with 34% broadband penetration. The county has no dedicated IT staff. All government operations run on spreadsheets. Residents have high trust in local government but low familiarity with digital services.

expected_outcome.notes: Low scores across all dimensions. Both paths should agree this community needs foundational work before AI adoption. The gap analysis here is the most detailed — use this last to show the action plan output. Note: high public_trust (52) is intentional — rural communities often trust local government even when infrastructure is low.
